narration: Your neighbor just pulled up in a brand new car — and without even knowing it, your brain started spending. That urge has a name, and a six-thousand-dollar price tag.
visual: Dark background. Two houses side by side. Neighbor's house has a gleaming amber car in driveway. Your house has a dull grey car. Envy arrow animates between them. Text: THE JONESES EFFECT.

narration: Studies show you're 40% more likely to make a major purchase within weeks of a neighbor or coworker buying something nice. Your brain is wired to close status gaps.
visual: Light background. Two stick figures side by side. One gets a star/upgrade badge, comparison arrow shoots to the other. Brain icon with 40% counter animating up. Red pulse on the second figure.

narration: That pressure adds up to over $6,200 a year for the average American. Invested instead over 30 years, that's a hundred and eighty-six thousand dollars gone.
visual: Dark background. Dollar counter animating from $0 to $6,200 with a year label. Then a 30-year bar growing to reveal $186,000. Piggy bank cracking open with coins spilling.

narration: Here's how it cascades. One neighbor gets a new car, so you feel your kitchen looks dated. New kitchen means the living room furniture looks wrong. It never stops.
visual: Light background. Chain reaction: car icon triggers arrow to house, arrow to sofa, arrow to TV, arrow to wallet emptying. Each icon animates in sequence with amber arrows connecting them.

narration: The move millionaires use is called anchor-switching. Instead of comparing up to people with more, deliberately compare down — and suddenly what you have feels like plenty.
visual: Dark background. Person figure in center. Arrow pointing up toward mansion and yacht labeled BROKE. Arrow pointing down toward small house labeled RICH FEELING. Choose the down arrow, it glows amber.

narration: Next time you feel that itch to upgrade, ask yourself: am I spending for me, or for them? That one question is worth $186,000. Follow for one money trick a day.
visual: Light background. Two-path sign: FOR THEM (red, money flying away) vs FOR ME (amber, piggy bank growing). Bold $186,000 text. Follow phone icon with subscribe CTA at bottom.
